# Azure Service Bus Connector - Full Documentation

# Azure Service Bus Connector 3.6 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The examples in this section show application flows that:

- [Sends messages to and receives messages](#send-receive) from Azure Service Bus
- [Sends and listens](#send-listen-ack) for messages and receives an acknowledgment using the
  `MANUAL` acknowledgment type
- [Sends and receives batch messages](#batch-send-receive)
- [Sends and receives messages using session IDs for session-aware queues](#send-receive-session-ids)
- [Using Azure Active Directory connector provider](#using-aad-connector)

These examples use variables for some field values. You can either:

- Replace the variables with their values in the code
- Provide the values for each variable in a properties file and then refer to that file from the
  connector configuration

  For more information about using a properties file, see
  [Configuring Property Placeholders](../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-app-properties-to-configure)

## Create a Mule Project

After you create a new Mule project and configure your credentials, you can create an application
flow for each example by using the provided XML code.

To create a Mule project:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.
3.  Create a file named `mule-app.properties` and save it in `<project-name>/src/main/resources`.
4.  Configure your Azure connection credentials in the file `mule-app.properties`, and include the
    name of the Azure queue you created, for example:

    #============================================================== #Connection Parameters
    #==============================================================

    # Azure common authentication configuration

    azure.namespace=myazure queue.name=mytestqueue

    # Shared Access Signature authentication

    azure.keyname=SAStokenname azure.key=SAStoken

    # Azure Active Directory authentication

    azure.tenantId=TenantId azure.clientId=ClientId azure.clientSecret=ClientSecret

5.  Save the file.
6.  Open the **Global Element Configuration** and click **Test Connection** to verify your
    credentials.

## Send and Receive Example

This flow shows an example of how to use the **Send** and **Receive From Queue** operations to send
and receive messages:

![Example Azure Service Bus flow that uses the Send and Receive From Queue operations](_images/azure-service-bus-send-receive.png)

To create this flow:

1.  In the Studio canvas, click **Configuration XML**.
2.  Delete all the contents after the line `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>`.
3.  Copy and paste the following XML after the line `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>`:

    ```xml
    <mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
    	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
    	xmlns:azure-service-bus-messaging="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-messaging" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
    	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
    	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-messaging http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-messaging/current/mule-azure-service-bus-messaging.xsd">
    	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties" doc:name="Configuration properties"/>
    	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="290d1aa8-2e61-4707-8f04-65f0e5e4cf07" >
    		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
    	</http:listener-config>
    	<azure-service-bus-messaging:config name="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" doc:name="Azure Service Bus Messaging Connector Config" doc:id="71d0e711-6d29-4eff-b640-00986a41dfbc" >
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:sas-connection namespace="${azure.namespace}" sharedAccessKeyName="${azure.keyname}" sharedAccessKey="${azure.key}" />
    	</azure-service-bus-messaging:config>
    	<flow name="send-flow" doc:id="2c8bce62-f192-4db2-9c70-792a5e78ac7f" >
    		<http:listener doc:name="Send Message Endpoint" doc:id="aa79c404-2e41-4453-9fde-18b8ea74c805" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/sendMessage" />
    		<ee:transform doc:name="Set Payload and Attributes" doc:id="bddebf8d-6d63-4db0-990f-94ecec23ddbd">
    			<ee:message>
    				<ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    {
    	key1: "value1",
    	key2: "value2 for Queue"
    }]]></ee:set-payload>
    			</ee:message>
    			<ee:variables>
    				<ee:set-variable variableName="messageAttributes"><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    {
    	messageId: "messageId1234",
    	correlationStrategy: "AUTO",
    	correlationId: "correlationId",
    	label: "my_label",
    	partitionKey: "the_partition_key",
    	timeToLive: 60,
    	ttlTimeUnit: "SECONDS",
    	scheduledEnqueueTimeUtc: "2018-12-09 03:01:00.000",
    	properties: {
    		property1: "value1",
    		property2: "value2"
    	}
    }]]></ee:set-variable>
    			</ee:variables>
    		</ee:transform>
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:send doc:name="Queue Send" doc:id="50b3592c-f777-4667-b042-00fd266120e8" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" destinationName="${queue.name}" messageId="#[vars.messageAttributes.messageId]" correlationId="#[vars.messageAttributes.correlationId]" label="#[vars.messageAttributes.label]" sendCorrelationStrategy="#[vars.messageAttributes.correlationStrategy]" partitionKey="#[vars.messageAttributes.partitionKey]" timeToLive="#[vars.messageAttributes.timeToLive]" scheduledEnqueueTimeUtc='#[vars.messageAttributes.scheduledEnqueueTimeUtc as LocalDateTime {format: "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS"}]' timeToLiveTimeUnit="#[vars.messageAttributes.ttlTimeUnit]" properties="#[vars.messageAttributes.properties]">
    		</azure-service-bus-messaging:send>
    	</flow>
    	<flow name="queue-receiver-flow" doc:id="9b39aaa5-e81f-4257-b65e-d4f48c8ef2d8" >
    		<http:listener doc:name="Receive From Queue" doc:id="386337a0-99c9-45cf-a81f-95d5e06f0b74" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/receive"/>
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:receive doc:name="Receive From Queue" doc:id="05c8c754-79f6-4873-8329-af7f48f01d3b" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" destinationName="${queue.name}"/>
    		<ee:transform doc:name="Attributes to Json" doc:id="a509a361-4bd7-4c6e-81cc-39ad36e7b625">
    			<ee:message>
    			</ee:message>
    			<ee:variables >
    				<ee:set-variable variableName="jsonAttributes" ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    attributes]]></ee:set-variable>
    			</ee:variables>
    		</ee:transform>
    		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log Attributes" doc:id="94e7254c-a197-4fa2-b437-b64719acb23d" message="#[vars.jsonAttributes]" />
    		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log the message body" doc:id="d802b081-f3fc-4ec2-a23c-96bc0db3863c" message="#[payload]" />
    	</flow>
    </mule>
    ```

4.  When you are prompted to regenerate `doc:id` values, click **Yes**.
5.  Save the project.
6.  Right-click in the Studio canvas and select **Run project <project-name>** to run the project.

## Batch Send and Receive Example

This application flow uses the **Send batch** and **Receive Batch** operations to send and receive
batches of messages to and from an Azure Service Bus queue.

The following screenshot shows the application flow for sending and receiving batch messages:

![Application flow that shows sending and receiving batch messages](_images/azure-service-bus-send-receive-batch.png)

To create this flow:

1.  In the Studio canvas, click **Configuration XML**.
2.  Delete all the contents after the line `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>`.
3.  Copy and paste the following XML after the line `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>`:

    ```xml
    <mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
    	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
    	xmlns:azure-service-bus-messaging="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-messaging" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
    	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
    	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-messaging http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-messaging/current/mule-azure-service-bus-messaging.xsd">
    	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties" doc:name="Configuration properties"/>
    	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="290d1aa8-2e61-4707-8f04-65f0e5e4cf07" >
    		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
    	</http:listener-config>
    	<azure-service-bus-messaging:config name="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" doc:name="Azure Service Bus Messaging Connector Config" doc:id="71d0e711-6d29-4eff-b640-00986a41dfbc" >
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:sas-connection namespace="${azure.namespace}" sharedAccessKeyName="${azure.keyname}" sharedAccessKey="${azure.key}" />
    	</azure-service-bus-messaging:config>
    	<flow name="send-batch-flow" doc:id="2c8bce62-f192-4db2-9c70-792a5e78ac7f" >
    		<http:listener doc:name="Send Message Endpoint" doc:id="aa79c404-2e41-4453-9fde-18b8ea74c805" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/sendBatch" />
    		<ee:transform doc:name="Messages with Json Bodies and Properties" doc:id="1f28f155-28c8-4feb-834c-0e790cac7a53" >
                    <ee:message >
                    </ee:message>
                    <ee:variables >
    					<ee:set-variable variableName="messages" ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
    output application/java
    ---
    [{
    	body: "{\"key1\":\"value1\",\"key2\":\"otherValue1\"}" as Binary,
    	messageId: "messageId1",
    	sendCorrelationStrategy: "AUTO",
    	correlationId: "correlation1",
    	contentType: "application/json",
    	replyToSessionId: "replyToSessionId1",
    	label: "label_msg1",
    	partitionKey: "the_partition_key1",
    	timeToLive: 240,
    	timeToLiveTimeUnit: "SECONDS",
    	zoneId: "America/Buenos_Aires",
    	scheduledEnqueueTimeUtc: "2018-12-09-00-0800" as LocalDateTime {format: "yyyy-MM-dd-HHZZZ"},
    	properties: {
    		property1: "value1",
    		property2: "value2"
    	},
    },
    {
    	body: "{\"key1\":\"value2\",\"key2\":\"otherValue2\"}" as Binary,
    	messageId: "messageId2",
    	sendCorrelationStrategy: "AUTO",
    	correlationId: "correlation2",
    	contentType: "application/json",
    	replyToSessionId: "replyToSessionId2",
    	label: "label_msg2",
    	partitionKey: "the_partition_key2",
    	timeToLive: 240,
    	timeToLiveTimeUnit: "SECONDS",
    	zoneId: "America/Buenos_Aires",
    	scheduledEnqueueTimeUtc: "2018-12-09-00-0800" as LocalDateTime {format: "yyyy-MM-dd-HHZZZ"},
    	properties: {
    		property1: "value1",
    		property2: "value2"
    	},
    },
    {
    	body: "{\"key1\":\"value3\",\"key2\":\"otherValue3\"}" as Binary,
    	messageId: "messageId3",
    	sendCorrelationStrategy: "AUTO",
    	correlationId: "correlation3",
    	contentType: "application/json",
    	replyToSessionId: "replyToSessionId3",
    	label: "label_msg3",
    	partitionKey: "the_partition_key3",
    	timeToLive: 240,
    	timeToLiveTimeUnit: "SECONDS",
    	zoneId: "America/Buenos_Aires",
    	scheduledEnqueueTimeUtc: "2018-12-09-00-0800" as LocalDateTime {format: "yyyy-MM-dd-HHZZZ"},
    	properties: {
    		property1: "value1",
    		property2: "value2"
    	},
    }]]]></ee:set-variable>
                    </ee:variables>
                </ee:transform>
    			<azure-service-bus-messaging:send-message-batch destinationName="${queue.name}" doc:name="Send batch of messages" doc:id="799d9ba3-68a9-4ef7-b518-6a715429b2a7" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config">
    				<azure-service-bus-messaging:messages ><![CDATA[#[vars.messages]]]></azure-service-bus-messaging:messages>
    			</azure-service-bus-messaging:send-message-batch>
    		<set-payload value='#[%dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    {
    	result: "Messages sent!"
    }]' doc:name="Set Payload" doc:id="c8b39b61-fa84-496d-b92c-e970a1a44003" />
    	</flow>
    	<flow name="queue-receiver-flow" doc:id="9b39aaa5-e81f-4257-b65e-d4f48c8ef2d8" >
    		<http:listener doc:name="Receive Batch From Queue" doc:id="386337a0-99c9-45cf-a81f-95d5e06f0b74" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/receiveBatch"/>
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:receive-batch doc:name="Receive Batch" doc:id="ee30123a-cc9f-4e08-a2e9-c41aede3923f" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" maxMessageCount="3" destinationName="${queue.name}"/>
    		<foreach doc:name="For Each" doc:id="16a42925-3142-48c7-9973-f951029af5fa" >
    			<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" doc:id="a5bfc3c6-2335-4c4e-845e-a67cf990a510" message='#["Received message number " ++ vars.counter]'/>
    			<ee:transform doc:name="Attributes to Json" doc:id="a509a361-4bd7-4c6e-81cc-39ad36e7b625">
    			<ee:message>
    			</ee:message>
    			<ee:variables>
    				<ee:set-variable variableName="jsonAttributes"><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    attributes]]></ee:set-variable>
    			</ee:variables>
    		</ee:transform>
    			<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log Attributes" doc:id="94e7254c-a197-4fa2-b437-b64719acb23d" message="#[vars.jsonAttributes]" />
    			<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log the message body" doc:id="d802b081-f3fc-4ec2-a23c-96bc0db3863c" message="#[%dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    payload]" />
    		</foreach>
    	</flow>
    </mule>
    ```

4.  Save the project.
5.  Right-click in the Studio canvas and select **Run project <project-name>** to run the project.

## Send, Listen, and Acknowledge the Message Example

This application flow uses the **Send** and **Message Listener** operations. The **Message
Listener** is using the `MANUAL` acknowledgment type, which means that the application logic is
responsible for acknowledging receipt of the message. In this example, that logic is provided by
adding the **Complete** operation to the same flow as the **Message Listener**.

The following screenshot shows this flow:

![Application flow that shows how the Send, Message Listener, and Complete operations are used](_images/azure-service-bus-send-listen-manual-ack.png)

To create this flow:

1.  In the Studio canvas, click **Configuration XML**.
2.  Delete all the contents after the line `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>`.
3.  Copy and paste the following XML after the line `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>`:

    ```xml
    <mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
    	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
    	  xmlns:azure-service-bus-messaging="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-messaging" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
    	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
    	  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-messaging http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-messaging/current/mule-azure-service-bus-messaging.xsd">
    	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties" doc:name="Configuration properties"/>
    	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="290d1aa8-2e61-4707-8f04-65f0e5e4cf07" >
    		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
    	</http:listener-config>
    	<azure-service-bus-messaging:config name="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" doc:name="Azure Service Bus Messaging Connector Config" doc:id="71d0e711-6d29-4eff-b640-00986a41dfbc" >
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:sas-connection namespace="${azure.namespace}" sharedAccessKeyName="${azure.keyname}" sharedAccessKey="${azure.key}" />
    	</azure-service-bus-messaging:config>
    	<flow name="send-endpoint-flow" doc:id="2c8bce62-f192-4db2-9c70-792a5e78ac7f" >
    		<http:listener doc:name="Send Queue Endpoint" doc:id="aa79c404-2e41-4453-9fde-18b8ea74c805" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/sendMessage" />
    		<ee:transform doc:name="Set Payload and Attributes" doc:id="bddebf8d-6d63-4db0-990f-94ecec23ddbd" >
    			<ee:message >
    				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    {
    	key1: "value1",
    	key2: "value2 for Queue"
    }]]></ee:set-payload>
    			</ee:message>
    			<ee:variables >
    				<ee:set-variable variableName="messageAttributes" ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    {
    	messageId: "messageId1234",
    	correlationStrategy: "AUTO",
    	correlationId: "correlationId",
    	label: "my_label",
    	partitionKey: "the_partition_key",
    	timeToLive: 60,
    	ttlTimeUnit: "SECONDS",
    	scheduledEnqueueTimeUtc: "2018-12-09 03:01:00.000",
    	properties: {
    		property1: "value1",
    		property2: "value2"
    	}
    }]]></ee:set-variable>
    			</ee:variables>
    		</ee:transform>
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:send doc:name="Queue Send" doc:id="50b3592c-f777-4667-b042-00fd266120e8" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" destinationName="${queue.name}" messageId='#[vars.messageAttributes.messageId]' correlationId="#[vars.messageAttributes.correlationId]" label="#[vars.messageAttributes.label]" sendCorrelationStrategy="#[vars.messageAttributes.correlationStrategy]" partitionKey="#[vars.messageAttributes.partitionKey]" timeToLive="#[vars.messageAttributes.timeToLive]" scheduledEnqueueTimeUtc='#[vars.messageAttributes.scheduledEnqueueTimeUtc  as LocalDateTime {format: "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS"}]' timeToLiveTimeUnit="#[vars.messageAttributes.ttlTimeUnit]" properties="#[vars.messageAttributes.properties]">
    		</azure-service-bus-messaging:send>
    	</flow>
    	<flow name="listener-manual-ack-flow" doc:id="541d4149-41b1-43e4-8d61-c16cf3ee502f" >
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:message-listener doc:name="Message listener" doc:id="cb61833e-9e1f-48b3-9430-a83b47c11a4c" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" ackMode="MANUAL" destinationName="${queue.name}" numberOfConsumers="1"/>
    		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log the message" doc:id="d802b081-f3fc-4ec2-a23c-96bc0db3863c" message="#[payload]"/>
    		<ee:transform doc:name="Attributes to Json" doc:id="a509a361-4bd7-4c6e-81cc-39ad36e7b625" >
    			<ee:message >
    				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
    output application/json
    ---
    attributes]]></ee:set-payload>
    			</ee:message>
    		</ee:transform>
    		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log Attributes" doc:id="94e7254c-a197-4fa2-b437-b64719acb23d" message="#[payload]"/>
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:complete-message doc:name="Ack of Message" doc:id="6aa7238b-3bc9-4a61-a39b-e1bd260ee7c5" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" lockToken="#[attributes.lockToken]" />
    		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Log Lock Token" doc:id="a91eea3e-231a-4675-9457-b6c7366ab647" message='#["LockToken = " ++ payload.lockToken]'/>
    	</flow>
    </mule>
    ```

4.  When you are prompted to regenerate `doc:id` values, click **Yes**.
5.  Save the project.
6.  Right-click in the Studio canvas and select **Run project <project-name>** to run the project.

## Send and Receive Messages Using Session IDs for Session-Aware Queues Example

This application flow uses the **Send** operation and **Message Listener** source. The flow is
configured to use session IDs for session-aware queues.

![Four flows that have Scheduler, Set Payload, and Send operations](_images/azure-service-bus-session-id-1.png)

![Flow that has first Message listener, Logger, Matches regex, another Logger, and error handling](_images/azure-service-bus-session-id-2.png)

![Flow that has second Message listener, Logger, Matches regex, another Logger, and error handling](_images/azure-service-bus-session-id-3.png)

![Flow that has third Message listener, Logger, Matches regex, another Logger, and error handling](_images/azure-service-bus-session-id-4.png)

To create this flow:

1.  In the Studio canvas, click **Configuration XML**.
2.  Delete all the contents after the line `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>`.
3.  Copy and paste the following XML after the line `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>`:

    ```xml
    <mule xmlns:json="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json"
    	  xmlns:validation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/validation"
    	  xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
    	  xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
    	  xmlns:azure-service-bus-messaging="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-messaging"
    	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
    	  xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/json/current/mule-json.xsd
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-messaging http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/azure-service-bus-messaging/current/mule-azure-service-bus-messaging.xsd
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/validation http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/validation/current/mule-validation.xsd">

    	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties" />

    	<!-- HTTP Listener Config for triggering send -->
    	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" >
    		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
    	</http:listener-config>

    	<!-- Azure Service Bus Messaging Connector Config -->
    	<azure-service-bus-messaging:config name="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config">
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:sas-connection namespace="${azure.namespace}" sharedAccessKeyName="${azure.keyname}" sharedAccessKey="${azure.key}" />
    	</azure-service-bus-messaging:config>

    	<!-- Flow to send a message with sessionId -->
    	<flow name="send-session-message-flow-1">
    		<scheduler doc:name="Scheduler" doc:id="85660b9c-19a0-4cb2-9dc0-1f07590d9e65" >
    			<scheduling-strategy >
    				<fixed-frequency frequency="30" timeUnit="SECONDS"/>
    			</scheduling-strategy>
    		</scheduler>
    		<set-payload value='#[%dw 2.0&#10;output application/json&#10;---&#10;{&#10; currentTime: now(),&#10; session: "${session.id1}",&#10;}]' doc:name="Set Payload" doc:id="390220c9-9480-47d0-838b-6d9ba921bddc" mimeType="application/json"/>
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:send
    				config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config"
    				destinationName="${queue.name}"
    				sessionId="${session.id1}">
    		</azure-service-bus-messaging:send>
    	</flow>

    	<!-- Flow to receive messages with sessionId -->
    	<flow name="send-session-message-flow-2" doc:id="20d400a8-fbb4-4fbf-b7de-662e0c96bc08">
    		<scheduler doc:name="Scheduler">
    			<scheduling-strategy>
    				<fixed-frequency frequency="30" timeUnit="SECONDS"/>
    			</scheduling-strategy>
    		</scheduler>
    		<set-payload value='#[%dw 2.0&#10;output application/json&#10;---&#10;{&#10; currentTime: now(),&#10; session: "${session.id2}",&#10;}]' doc:name="Set Payload" doc:id="2490c774-3e34-4e11-8931-5feb1f8f6ac7" mimeType="application/json" />
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:send destinationName="${queue.name}" doc:name="Send" doc:id="a12f8163-3260-411f-ae43-2dfc3b01a3eb" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" sessionId="${session.id2}" />
    	</flow>

    	<flow name="send-session-message-flow-3" doc:id="38c54914-a253-4d4c-ad81-166b8cde6eeb">
    		<scheduler doc:name="Scheduler">
    			<scheduling-strategy>
    				<fixed-frequency frequency="30" timeUnit="SECONDS"/>
    			</scheduling-strategy>
    		</scheduler>
    		<set-payload value='#[%dw 2.0&#10;output application/json&#10;---&#10;{&#10; currentTime: now(),&#10; session: "${session.id3}",&#10;}]' doc:name="Set Payload" doc:id="439e969a-ec0c-447d-ae7a-636526e3871d" mimeType="application/json" />
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:send destinationName="${queue.name}" doc:name="Send" doc:id="0e6d84ff-8ec2-488f-8ac7-cb7a8ac1844b" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" sessionId="${session.id3}" />
    	</flow>


    	<flow name="send-session-message-flow-4" doc:id="0f0ca780-444d-448f-8abf-5c63d3ec42a7">
    		<scheduler doc:name="Scheduler" doc:id="e3605391-476d-486a-b183-341fd84f4dbe">
    			<scheduling-strategy>
    				<fixed-frequency frequency="30" timeUnit="SECONDS" />
    			</scheduling-strategy>
    		</scheduler>
    		<set-payload value='#[%dw 2.0&#10;output application/json&#10;---&#10;{&#10; currentTime: now(),&#10; session: "${session.id4}",&#10;}]' doc:name="Set Payload" doc:id="9ec14ba0-3fe9-48e2-b493-f6414b601064" mimeType="application/json" />
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:send destinationName="${queue.name}" doc:name="Send" doc:id="5347dc2f-f93c-4095-8c10-6f56fd8342c8" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" sessionId="${session.id4}" />
    	</flow>
    	<flow name="fifo-listener-1">
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:message-listener
    				config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config"
    				ackMode="AUTO" doc:name="Message listener-1" sessionID="${session.id1}" numberOfConsumers="1" destinationName="${queue.name}">
    			<reconnect-forever />
    			<azure-service-bus-messaging:response timeToLive="1" timeToLiveTimeUnit="HOURS" messageId="1" correlationId="1">
    			</azure-service-bus-messaging:response>
    		</azure-service-bus-messaging:message-listener>
    		<try doc:name="Try">
    			<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" doc:id="24c6b101-b7d3-4b7d-bbc5-f352617eee17" message='"listener1: #[payload]"' />
    			<validation:matches-regex doc:name="Matches regex1" doc:id="5810c860-a570-42bd-91cd-282150fea2c7" value="#[payload.session]" regex="${session.id1}" message="Error on ${session.id1}" />
    			<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger1" doc:id="0a0d03fa-1a8e-41be-a747-62fab5924d0c" message="#[payload]" />
    		</try>
    		<error-handler>
    			<on-error-continue>
    				<raise-error type="VALIDATION1:VALIDATION_ERROR1" doc:name="Raise Error">
    				</raise-error>
    			</on-error-continue>
    		</error-handler>
    	</flow>
    	<flow name="fifo-listener-2" doc:id="4d3e547e-77c2-4760-b760-6930d8b93c9e">
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:message-listener ackMode="AUTO" destinationName="${queue.name}" doc:name="Message listener-2" doc:id="01832bbd-d2eb-4fb9-8ed1-a90587874a8b" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" numberOfConsumers="1" sessionID="${session.id2}">
    			<reconnect-forever />
    		</azure-service-bus-messaging:message-listener>
    		<try doc:name="Try">
    			<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger1" doc:id="cf0e243b-5188-4418-abc1-a60275915adc" message='"listener2: #[payload]"' />
    			<validation:matches-regex doc:name="Matches regex" doc:id="05e5c2df-cf96-4bfe-a7b6-da22f09dd238" value="#[payload.session]" regex="${session.id2}" message="Error on ${session.id2}"/>
    			<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" doc:id="54b7b482-9c24-416e-8c46-c717af00acb0" message="#[payload]" />
    		</try>
    		<error-handler>
    			<on-error-continue>
    				<raise-error type="VALIDATION2:VALIDATION_ERROR2" doc:name="Raise Error">
    				</raise-error>
    			</on-error-continue>
    		</error-handler>
    	</flow>
    	<flow name="fifo-listener-3" doc:id="3ecafefc-c19d-485a-8c6a-45d79c7b5cad">
    		<azure-service-bus-messaging:message-listener ackMode="AUTO" destinationName="${queue.name}" doc:name="Message listener-3" doc:id="3b6fa3d6-f8cf-4191-bbb2-29208aae7d5b" config-ref="Azure_Service_Bus_Messaging_Connector_Config" numberOfConsumers="1" sessionID="${session.id3}">
    			<reconnect-forever />
    		</azure-service-bus-messaging:message-listener>
    		<try doc:name="Try">
    			<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger1" doc:id="6b9ee655-0395-44f7-8322-36b0e77643b9" message='"listener3: #[payload]"' />
    			<validation:matches-regex doc:name="Matches regex" doc:id="de2db011-3a58-48e0-8dcf-222d5da01a22" value="#[payload.session]" regex="${session.id3}" message="Error on ${session.id3}"/>
    			<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" doc:id="fd1ad726-ff8f-4cff-9daa-726cb5e5d014" message="#[payload]" />
    		</try>
    		<error-handler>
    			<on-error-continue>
    				<raise-error type="VALIDATION3:VALIDATION_ERROR3" doc:name="Raise Error">
    				</raise-error>
    			</on-error-continue>
    		</error-handler>
    	</flow>
    </mule>
    ```

4.  When you are prompted to regenerate `doc:id` values, click **Yes**.
5.  Save the project.
6.  Right-click in the Studio canvas and select **Run project <project-name>** to run the project.

## Using Azure Active Directory Connector Provider

Some organizations use Azure Active Directory to authenticate their instances. Any of the previous
examples can be modified to support this scenario. Replace the Shared Access Signature
authentication with the Azure Active Directory authentication:

```xml
<azure-service-bus-messaging:sas-connection namespace="${azure.namespace}" sharedAccessKeyName="${azure.keyname}" sharedAccessKey="${azure.key}" />
```

```xml
<azure-service-bus-messaging:aad-connection namespace="${azure.namespace}" tenantId="${azure.tenantId}" clientId="${azure.clientId}" clientSecret="${azure.clientSecret}" />
```

Ensure that:

- Your `mule-app.properties` file contains the **Tenant Id**, **Client Id**, and **Client Secret**
  fields.
- Your app registration contains the proper
  [permissions and roles](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/service-bus-messaging/authenticate-application).

## See Also

- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Azure Service Bus Connector 3.6 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Azure Service Bus (Azure Service Bus Connector) enables message integration
with Azure Service Bus on the cloud.

---

## Configurations

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#config_sas-connection">Shared Access Signature</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#config_aad-connection">Azure Active Directory</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Correlation Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to specify a correlation ID when publishing messages. This applies both for custom correlation IDs specified at the operation level and for default correlation IDs taken from the current event.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Zone Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Zone ID property is used to identify the rules used to convert between an Instant and LocalDateTime. If the Zone ID equals <code>Z</code>, UTC is used. If the Zone ID is not specified, the system default Zone ID is used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the instance expires at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Shared Access Signature

The SAS connection type uses the SAS Key Name and the SAS Key to generate a token, which is used by
the Azure Service Bus SDK to establish the connection to the Service Bus instance.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Service namespace</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The namespace contains the resources to which the connector can establish a connection.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Shared access key name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the Shared Access key</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Shared access key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Shared Access key</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Security</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>TLS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>TLS enabled by default, using the default JVM certificates to secure communication.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of time to wait for the operation to execute before throwing an error. This value is qualified by the Response Timeout TimeUnit.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Minimum Retry Backoff</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The minimum backoff interval for the retry. This must be a positive integer and setting it to zero results in ineffective retry behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Minimum Retry Backoff Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the Minimum Retry Backoff value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum Retry Backoff</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum backoff interval for the retry</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum Retry Backoff Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit which qualifies the Maximum Retry Backoff value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Retries</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of retries that the connector will attempt</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>10</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Increased Wait On Server Busy (Deprecated)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of additional time to wait whenever thereâs a ServerBusyException</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Increased Wait On Server Busy Time Unit (Deprecated)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit which qualifies the Increased wait on server busy value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cache Producers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When checked the producer for a given destination is cached</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Azure Active Directory

The Azure Active Directory (Azure AD) connection type provides role-based access control (RBAC), in
which users can use
[Azure AD app registrations](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/service-bus-messaging/authenticate-application)
to generate a **Client Id** and a **Client Secret** for authentication.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Service namespace</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Namespace that contains resources to which the connector establishes a connection.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Azure endpoint</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Constructs the complete URL that the connector uses to establish a connection.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>servicebus.windows.net</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tenant Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Tenant in which the app registration and the Azure Service Bus instance are running.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the app registration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secret key of the app registration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Security</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>TLS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>TLS enabled by default, using the default JVM certificates to secure communication.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long to wait for an operation to execute before throwing an error.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Response Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Minimum Retry Backoff</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Minimum backoff interval for the retry. This must be a positive integer and setting it to zero results in ineffective retry behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Minimum Retry Backoff Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Minimum Retry Backoff</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum Retry Backoff</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum backoff interval for the retry.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>30</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum Retry Backoff Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Maximum Retry Backoff</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Retries</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of retries that the connector attempts.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>10</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Increased Wait On Server Busy (Deprecated)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of additional time to wait whenever thereâs a ServerBusyException</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Increased Wait On Server Busy Time Unit (Deprecated)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit which qualifies the Increased wait on server busy value</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cache Producers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When checked the producer for a given destination is cached</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## List of Operations

- [Abandon](#abandonMessage)
- [Complete](#completeMessage)
- [Receive](#receive)
- [Receive Batch](#receiveBatch)
- [Send](#send)
- [Send batch of messages](#sendMessageBatch)

## List of Sources

- [Message Listener](#message-listener)

## Operations

### Abandon

`<azure-service-bus-messaging:abandon-message>`

Abandon message stored in the current receiver (This is equivalent to a NACK operation). This
operation can be used only from inside a flow that also includes a **Message Listener Source**
operation.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Lock Token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Lock Token from the message to abandon. This represents a UUID for a message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#config)

#### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:OPERATION_TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:TOKEN_NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:ANY

### Complete

`<azure-service-bus-messaging:complete-message>`

This operation completes a message received from within a flow with a Message Listener operation in
the current source. This is the same as an acknowledge.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Lock Token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Lock Token from the message to ACK. This represents a UUID for a message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#config)

#### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:TOKEN_EXPIRED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:OPERATION_TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:ANY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:TOKEN_NOT_FOUND

### Receive

`<azure-service-bus-messaging:receive>`

This operation receives a message from a specific queue or subscription. In this version of the
connector, only Immediate Receive Mode (`RECEIVEANDDELETE`) is supported.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the destination from which messages are received</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subscription Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the subscription from which messages are received</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NONE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AttributesWithoutLockToken">Attributes Without Lock Token</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#config)

#### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:OPERATION_TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:INVALID_SIGNATURE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:NOT_ENOUGH_PERMITS
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:DESTINATION_NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:ANY

### Receive Batch

`<azure-service-bus-messaging:receive-batch>`

This operation attempts to receive up to maxMessageCount messages from a specific queue or
subscription. In this version of the connector, only Immediate Receive Mode (`RECEIVEANDDELETE`) is
supported.

Due to the design of the Azure-provided SDK that Azure Service Bus Connector uses, the actual number
of messages received may be fewer or more than the maxMessageCount specified. This behavior occurs
because the Azure-provided SDK treats maxMessageCount as an approximation rather than a strict
limit. Additionally, a null value may be returned if the operation exceeds the specified timeout or
if there are no more messages to receive.

> [!IMPORTANT] To ensure that all available messages are received, you might need to call this
> operation multiple times.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Message Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of messages that can be received</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the destination from which messages are received</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subscription Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the subscription from which messages are received</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NONE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of a variable in which to store the operationâs output</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of [Binary] payload and [<a href="#AttributesWithoutLockToken">Attributes Without Lock Token</a>] attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#config)

#### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:OPERATION_TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:INVALID_SIGNATURE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:NOT_ENOUGH_PERMITS
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:DESTINATION_NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:ANY

### Send

`<azure-service-bus-messaging:send>`

This operation sends a message to a Queue or Topic.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Destination name to which to send the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Body</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Body of this message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message identifier is an application-defined value that uniquely identifies the message and its payload</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Correlation Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Lists the possible strategies to determine the correlation ID to send on an outbound operation that supports correlation</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Correlation Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Allows an application to specify a context for the message for the purposes of correlation</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Session Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Session identifier for a session-aware entity</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reply To</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the destination to which to send the message reply</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reply To Session Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The ID of the Session associated with the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Label</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables the application to indicate the purpose of the message to the receiver</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partition Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>For partitioned entities, setting this value enables assigning related messages to the same internal partition, so that the submission sequence order is correctly recorded.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Relative duration, in milliseconds, after which the message expires, starting from the instant the message is accepted and stored by the broker</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the Write Concern Timeout</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheduled Enqueue Time Utc</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the UTC instant at which the message is logically enqueued, sequenced, and therefore made available for retrieval</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of user application properties set on the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#config)

#### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:PAYLOAD_SIZE_EXCEEDED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:OPERATION_TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:INVALID_SIGNATURE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:NOT_ENOUGH_PERMITS
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:DESTINATION_NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:INVALID_PARAMS
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:ANY

### Send batch of messages

`<azure-service-bus-messaging:send-message-batch>`

This operation sends a list of messages to a queue or topic.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Destination name to which to send the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Messages</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Messages to send</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#config)

#### Throws

- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:PAYLOAD_SIZE_EXCEEDED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:OPERATION_TIMEOUT
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:INVALID_SIGNATURE
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:ANY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:NOT_ENOUGH_PERMITS
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:CONNECTIVITY
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:DESTINATION_NOT_FOUND
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- AZURE-SERVICE-BUS-MESSAGING:INVALID_PARAMS

## Sources

### Message Listener

`<azure-service-bus-messaging:message-listener>`

This operation receives messages from a specific queue or subscription.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Session Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Message session ID.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Acknowledgment mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>MANUAL</p></li><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the way to acknowledge received messages. AUTO and MANUAL uses the PeekLock mode. IMMEDIATE uses the ReceiveAndDelete mode.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefetch Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When Prefetch is enabled, the receiver quietly acquires more messages, up to the PrefetchCount limit, beyond what the application initially requested. Setting this value to zero (<code>0</code>) disables prefetching.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Number Of Consumers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of concurrent consumers to use to receive messages</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffered Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Buffered is the default mode. Consumers send the message to the flow and immediately receive the next message. If Buffered Mode is disabled, the consumers do not receive the next message until the current message exits the flow.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enable Auto Lock Renewal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies whether the source should automatically renew message locks for session-aware queues, which prevents lock expiration during long-running message processing. The message lock renewal duration defaults to 5 minutes (hardcoded per Azure SDK). Flows exceeding 5 minutes of processing time experience lock expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Mime Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Output Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether this source should be executed only on the primary node when running in a cluster</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Destination Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the destination from which messages are received</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subscription Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the subscription from which messages are received</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NONE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Body</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Body of the message</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>#[payload]</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The message identifier is an application-defined value that uniquely identifies the message and its payload.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Correlation Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>AUTO</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Correlation Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Allows an application to specify a context for the message for the purposes of correlation</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Session Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Session identifier for a session-aware entity</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reply To</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Standard way to express a reply path to the receiver of the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reply To Session Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Session identifier for a session-aware entity</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Label</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables the application to indicate the purpose of the message to the receiver</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partition Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>For partitioned entities, setting this value enables assigning related messages to the same internal partition, so that the submission sequence order is correctly recorded.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This value is the relative duration after which the message expires, starting from the instant the message is accepted and stored by the broker</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the Write Concern Timeout</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheduled Enqueue Time Utc</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the UTC instant at which the message is logically enqueued, sequenced, and made available for retrieval</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of user application properties set on the message</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#AzureMessageAttributes">Azure Message Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#config)

## Types

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts to make</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance is allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Azure Message Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Correlation Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies a context for the message for correlation purposes</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Dead Letter Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Read-only property that indicates the entity in which the message was unable to be delivered</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of times message delivery was attempted. When a message lock expires, or when the message is abandoned by the receiver, delivery count increases.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enqueued Time Utc</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The instant in UTC time when the message is accepted and stored in the entity</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expires At</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The instant in UTC time when the message is marked for removal and no longer able to be retrieved from the entity because of its expiration, which is controlled by <strong>Time to Live</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Label</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>States the purpose of the message to the receiver</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Lock Token</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Read-only property that references the lock that is in peek-lock receive mode</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Locked Until Utc</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>UTC instant until which the message is kept locked in the queue or subscription</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Uniquely identifies the message and its payload</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partition Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Used for partitioned entities to enable related messages to be assigned to the same internal partition so that submission sequence order of the messages is recorded correctly</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reply To</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path for where the receiver of the message can reply</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reply To Session Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specify the specific session ID for where the application can receive messages</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheduled Enqueue Time Utc</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Schedule the UTC time for when the message will appear in the queue</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sequence Number</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique 64-bit integer that is assigned to a message when the message is accepted and stored by the broker, and which functions as the messageâs true ID</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Session Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the session association of the message for session-aware entities</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time To Live</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Relative duration after which time the message expires, starting at the moment the message is accepted and stored by the broker, as defined in Enqueued Time UTC</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reserved for future use in routing scenarios and currently ignored by the broker.
